title: Remove A11y icon from header, darken all text site-wide for high-contrast readability

requirements.text: Remove the Accessibility (A11y) icon/button from the header's icon row — the one positioned between the Language (EN) switcher and the AI Chatbot (DSOUZA AI) icon. After removal, adjust spacing and alignment of the remaining header controls (Size, EN, DSOUZA AI, Voice) so the layout is clean, symmetrical, and professionally aligned on desktop, tablet, and mobile.

quotes: Remove the Accessibility (A11y) icon located between the Language (EN) option and the AI Chatbot (DSOUZA AI) icon in the header.
Adjust spacing after removing the icon so the layout remains clean, symmetrical, and professionally aligned.

acceptanceCriteria: The A11y icon is no longer visible in the header on any viewport size.
Remaining header controls (Size, EN, DSOUZA AI, Voice) are evenly spaced and visually balanced after removal.
No broken layout, gaps, or misalignment on desktop, tablet, or mobile.

requirements.text: Make all header text labels — Size, EN, DSOUZA AI, and Voice — darker and highly readable. Apply a dark color of #000000 or #1A1A1A so they stand out clearly against the light header background.

quotes: Make all header text labels (Size, EN, DSOUZA AI, Voice) darker and highly readable.

acceptanceCriteria: All text labels in the header icon/control row render in #000000 or #1A1A1A.
Labels are clearly legible against the header background at all viewport sizes.
Contrast ratio meets WCAG AA standard (minimum 4.5:1 for normal text).

requirements.text: Apply high-contrast dark text colors across the entire website. Use #000000 for primary text and #1A1A1A or #222222 for secondary text. This applies to all headings (h1–h6), subheadings, navigation menu items, body text, button labels, card text, form labels, and footer text. Update global CSS custom properties and/or Tailwind config to enforce these values site-wide. Maintain the light, premium background; only darken foreground text.

quotes: Make all text across the entire website darker — including headings, subheadings, menu items, body text, buttons, and footer text.
Ensure accessibility compliance with strong contrast ratios (WCAG standards).
Maintain a light, premium, modern digital banking theme while improving readability.

acceptanceCriteria: All page headings and subheadings render in #000000 or a near-black equivalent.
All body/paragraph text renders in #000000 or #1A1A1A.
Navigation menu items (desktop and mobile) render in high-contrast dark text.
Button labels are dark and readable (meeting WCAG AA contrast on their backgrounds).
Footer text (links, copyright, labels) is rendered in dark, high-contrast color.
No text element across Home, Services, About, Neo, or Contact pages uses a light-grey or low-contrast color that fails WCAG AA.
The soft light background of the site is preserved; only text foreground colors are darkened.
Layout and design remain elegant, minimal, and suitable for a professional banking platform.
All changes are responsive and look correct on desktop, tablet, and mobile.

constraints: Do not alter the backend — this is a frontend-only change.
Do not remove the accessibility font-size (Size) control or the high-contrast toggle functionality — only remove the A11y icon/button from the header UI.
Preserve the light, premium background theme; only darken foreground text colors.
Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
Maintain full responsiveness across desktop, tablet, and mobile.
nonGoals: Adding new features or sections to any page.
Changing the background color or overall light theme of the site.
Modifying backend logic or data models.
Redesigning the header layout beyond spacing adjustment after icon removal.
Changing font families or font sizes globally.
